title: The 4 parts of a good prompt
content:
## Learning goal
To understand the 4 parts of a good prompt and how to use them in writing clear and effective prompts.

## Who
Students interested in AI and natural language processing.

## What
The 4 parts of a good prompt include:
1. **Goal** - Clearly state what you want.
2. **Context** - Provide background information if needed.
3. **Format** - Use bullet points, tables, or short paragraphs.
4. **Style** - Use a formal, friendly, or technical tone as needed.

## Where
In your writing and learning process.

## When
When you want to create clear and effective prompts.

## Why it matters
Ambiguity = random answers.
Structure makes AI responses usable and consistent.

## How
1. Use the 4-part structure.
2. Write 2 prompts that are clear and cite-ready.
3. Generate outputs; check if they match your intent.

## Guided exercise (10–15 min)
Write 2 prompts with all 4 parts and generate outputs to check if they match your intent.

## Independent exercise (5–10 min)
Refine one prompt to be more specific by adding data, format, or constraints.

## Self-check
Verify that both prompts include goal, context, format, and style.

## Bibliography (sources used)
None

## Read more (optional)
OpenAI Prompt Guide: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering
AI 30-Day – Day 2: The 4 parts of a good prompt

emailBody:
## Today
Short localized summary with one clear call to action.

## Introduction
Welcome to Day 2 of the AI 30-Day course. In this lesson, you'll learn about the 4 parts of a good prompt and how to use them in writing clear and effective prompts.

## What to Expect
In this lesson, you'll learn about the 4 parts of a good prompt and how to use them in writing clear and effective prompts. You'll also practice writing two prompts with all four parts and generating outputs to check if they match your intent.

## Get Started
To get started, open the guided exercise and write two prompts with all four parts. Then, generate outputs and check if they match your intent.

## Next Steps
After completing this lesson, you'll be able to write clear and effective prompts using the 4-part structure. You'll also be able to refine one prompt to be more specific by adding data, format, or constraints.

## Resources
For more information on prompt engineering, check out the OpenAI Prompt Guide: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering

## Conclusion
Congratulations on completing Day 2 of the AI 30-Day course. You've learned about the 4 parts of a good prompt and how to use them in writing clear and effective prompts.

## Call to Action
Open the lesson to continue learning.
